title: Grill Caddy with Paper Towel Holder
summary: A small carry caddy for grilling tools with a built-in dowel paper towel holder — cut from a single cedar fence picket.
description:
This caddy is a tiny, genuinely useful build: a slatted box with an upright handle end and a dowel rod through the opposite end to hold a paper towel roll, sized to carry tongs, a spatula, and condiments out to the grill in one trip. Cedar fence pickets are the whole material list here — one picket yields nearly the entire project with a small amount left over.

Build the box first from the uprights, bottom, end, and sides, then drill matching holes through the two uprights for the dowel rod. The dowel is held with a screw driven in from the outside of each upright rather than glue, since a paper towel holder needs to be sturdy but not permanent — a snapped-off dowel should be replaceable without rebuilding the caddy.

This is a genuinely quick, low-stakes build: sand thoroughly since it will be handled constantly and used outdoors, and finish with a stain rated for exterior use since it will live near a hot grill in variable weather.

cutList.note: Cut to fit the caddy's actual inside width.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Attach the bottom to the two uprights and the end piece with brad nails and exterior wood glue, forming the caddy's main box shape.

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Attach the two side pieces to the box, following the same box shape.

steps.title: Add the dowel holder
steps.body: Drill matching holes through both uprights near the open end for the dowel rod. Cut the dowel to fit the caddy's actual inside width and insert. Secure each end with a screw driven in from the outside of the upright rather than glue, so the dowel can be replaced if it breaks.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand thoroughly, since the caddy will be handled constantly and used outdoors. Apply an exterior-rated wood stain.

unresolvedImages.alt: Cedar grill tool caddy with a dowel paper towel holder
